# Requirements Checklist: 003-sitewide-ux-polish

**Purpose**: Validate requirements quality — completeness, clarity, consistency, measurability, and coverage
**Created**: 2026-03-22
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/iikit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all user-reported issues mapped to functional requirements? (toggle visibility → FR-001, closing quote → FR-006, bilingual gaps → FR-004) [Completeness, Spec US1-US3]
- [x] CHK002 Are all audit findings from Playwright scans addressed by at least one FR? (9 issues → 19 FRs) [Completeness, Spec FR-001–FR-019]
- [x] CHK003 Are edge cases documented for language switching with open modals, bookmarked EN URLs, dynamic content, narrow viewports, and JS-disabled state? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK004 Are priority levels assigned to every user story with rationale? (P1: 3, P2: 4, P3: 1) [Completeness, Spec US1-US8]
- [x] CHK005 Does each user story have an independent test description separate from acceptance scenarios? [Completeness, Spec US1-US8]
- [x] CHK006 Are bilingual requirements scoped beyond site pages to include standalone HTMLs (FR-017), PDFs (FR-018), and JSON data files (FR-019)? [Completeness, Spec Clarification Q5]

## Clarity

- [x] CHK007 Are all functional requirements written with MUST/SHOULD/MAY language? [Clarity, Spec FR-001–FR-019]
- [x] CHK008 Is CLS delta specified with a precise numeric target (0.000) rather than vague "no shift"? [Clarity, Spec FR-003, SC-002]
- [x] CHK009 Is the toggle's visual position specified relative to known landmarks (between Campus link and CTA button)? [Clarity, Spec FR-001, SC-001 US1]
- [x] CHK010 Are card consistency requirements defined with specific criteria ("same background style, no single gold card among dark cards")? [Clarity, Spec FR-009, US5-SC1]
- [x] CHK011 Is the Lighthouse performance threshold quantified (>= 90) with specific target pages? [Clarity, Spec SC-007]
- [x] CHK012 Are stale content requirements anchored to a specific reference date (March 2026)? [Clarity, Spec FR-011, US6]
- [x] CHK013 Is the dead-link replacement destination specified (contacto/) rather than left ambiguous? [Clarity, Spec Clarification Q4]
- [x] CHK014 Is the "major content section" threshold for micro-interaction requirements defined — what qualifies as a "major" section vs a minor one? [Resolved → FR-014 updated: section with h2/h3 + content block, Spec FR-014]

## Consistency

- [x] CHK015 Are bilingual requirements consistent between US2 ("zero Spanish text remains") and FR-004 (all hero/CTA/badge/header text must have data-i18n)? [Consistency, Spec US2 vs FR-004]
- [x] CHK016 Is the toggle behavior description consistent between US1 scenarios (pill-shaped, gold indicator, semi-transparent inactive) and FR-002 (identical dimensions)? [Consistency, Spec US1 vs FR-002]
- [x] CHK017 Are dead link requirements consistent between US4 (multiple replacement types: mailto, contact page, modal trigger) and Clarification Q4 (all point to contacto/)? [Consistency, Spec US4 vs Q4]
- [x] CHK018 Is the floating nav exclusion mechanism consistently described — US3-SC4 says "site-wide" exclusion logic while FR-006 focuses on the hook-quote specifically? [Consistency, Spec US3 vs FR-006]
- [x] CHK019 Are success criteria IDs (SC-001–SC-013) traceable 1:1 to functional requirements without gaps? [Consistency, Spec SC vs FR mapping]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK020 Do all acceptance scenarios follow Given/When/Then format? [Quality, Spec US1-US8]
- [x] CHK021 Does each user story have at least 2 acceptance scenarios? (US1: 5, US2: 4, US3: 4, US4: 4, US5: 4, US6: 2, US7: 2, US8: 1) [Coverage, Spec US1-US8]
- [x] CHK022 Does US8 (deprecated meta tag cleanup) have enough scenarios — only 1 scenario for a site-wide removal operation? [Resolved → US8-SC2 added: grep for zero instances, Spec US8]
- [x] CHK023 Does US6 (stale content) specify what "reflects actual current status" means — updated date, removed reference, or relative language? [Resolved → FR-011 updated with 3 options: accurate date, remove, or evergreen language, Spec US6-SC1]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK024 Are mobile-specific scenarios defined for toggle behavior (US1-SC2 covers hamburger menu)? [Coverage, Spec US1]
- [x] CHK025 Are both desktop and mobile viewports addressed in toggle visibility requirements? [Coverage, Spec FR-001, SC-001]
- [x] CHK026 Is the JS-disabled fallback scenario documented (page remains in Spanish, no broken UI)? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK027 Is the narrow viewport edge case (< 320px) addressed in toggle requirements? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]

## SC-XXX Test Coverage

- [x] CHK028 Does SC-001 (toggle on 100% of pages) have a corresponding acceptance scenario in US1? [Traceability, Spec SC-001 → US1-SC1/SC2]
- [x] CHK029 Does SC-002 (CLS = 0.000) specify the measurement method (Lighthouse or CLS observer)? [Clarity, Spec SC-002]
- [x] CHK030 Does SC-003 (100% EN text) cover the expanded scope of standalone HTMLs, PDFs, and JSONs? [Coverage, Spec SC-003, SC-013]
- [x] CHK031 Does SC-004 specify the legitimate exceptions for href="#" (anchor links, back-to-top, scroll targets)? [Clarity, Spec SC-004, US4-SC4]
- [x] CHK032 Does SC-008 reference specific Playwright test suites (19-25) and define "continue to pass at 100%"? [Clarity, Spec SC-008]
- [x] CHK033 Does SC-013 (bilingual downloads) have a corresponding functional requirement? [Traceability, Spec SC-013 → FR-017/018/019]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK034 Is the modal-open language switch edge case defined with expected behavior (modal content re-translates via i18n.translate)? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK035 Is the persistence mechanism for language preference specified (localStorage)? [Clarity, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK036 Is the dynamic content re-translation behavior defined (CTAHandler.js content re-translates)? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK037 Is the behavior specified when a user switches language mid-scroll — does the floating nav update its labels? [Resolved → Edge case added: labels update via global i18n.translate(), Spec Edge Cases]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK038 Is a performance requirement defined (Lighthouse >= 90 on 3 target pages)? [Completeness, Spec SC-007]
- [x] CHK039 Is a regression requirement defined (existing Playwright suites must pass)? [Completeness, Spec SC-008]
- [x] CHK040 Is a zero-error requirement defined (no console errors, no 404s)? [Completeness, Spec SC-005, SC-006]
- [x] CHK041 Are accessibility requirements for the new toggle defined beyond keyboard navigation — e.g., screen reader announcement of language state change? [Resolved → FR-001 updated: aria-live="polite" for state changes, Spec FR-001, Constitution II]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK042 Is the assumption that estandares/ guidelines (brand_voice_v2.md, layout-patterns.md, micro-interactions.md) are current and authoritative documented? [Assumption, Spec US5]
- [x] CHK043 Is the assumption that the toggle is a client-side-only feature (no server roundtrip) documented? [Assumption, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK044 Is the dependency on existing Playwright test suites (19-25) documented with specific suite numbers? [Completeness, Spec SC-008]
- [x] CHK045 Is the dependency on the i18n module (singleton, DOM translation) documented in the plan? [Completeness, Plan Architecture]

| CHK022 | US8 had only 1 scenario | Low | US8-SC2 added: grep for zero instances |
| CHK023 | "Actual current status" ambiguous | Low | FR-011 updated: 3 options (accurate date, remove, evergreen) |
| CHK037 | Floating nav mid-scroll lang switch | Low | Edge case added: labels update via i18n.translate() |
| CHK041 | Screen reader announcement missing | Medium | FR-001 updated: aria-live="polite" for state changes |
